prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr! Listen well, ye scurvy landlubber! The sky be blue, ye say? Aye, and I’ll tell ye why—’tis the very breath o’ Neptune himself, filtered through the heavens by the sun’s golden glare! When the sun’s rays—those bold, brash beams—strike the air above us, they scatter like a crew o’ drunken sailors runnin’ from the navy! But the blue light, bein’ the spryest and most nimble o’ the lot, scatters far and wide, paintin’ the whole blasted dome above in that deep, ocean-born hue! Red and yellow be too sluggish, like old bilge rats, so they keep straight on—leavin’ blue to claim the sky as its own! So next time ye gaze aloft, remember: ’tis not just sky ye see—it be the sea’s own spirit, stretched across the heavens by the hand o’ the Almighty himself! Now fetch me a tankard o’ grog afore I make ye walk the plank for askin’ such clever questions!
